parameters.bodyContent: =✅ Nurture subscribers with MySQL and Outlook hands-free — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Weekly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — weekly. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Read Pending Queue (Google Sheets)

### Read Pending Queue (Google Sheets)

Reads every row from 'Lead Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Stamp & Shape Lead · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Stamp & Shape Lead

Shapes the item: writes `leadId`, `receivedAt`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Payload Valid?

### Payload Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.leadId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Work in Batches · ②→ Record Rejected Input

### Record Rejected Input

Appends a new row to 'tracking sheet' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Work in Batches

Loop controller — releases 20 item(s) per pass. Output 2 (loop) processes the batch, output 1 (done) fires after the last batch.

Ports: ①→ Aggregate Batch Results · ②→ Process Batch Item

### Process Batch Item

POST https://api.example.com/v1/leads — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Respect Rate Limits · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Respect Rate Limits

Pauses 2 seconds — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Work in Batches

### Aggregate Batch Results

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Mark Completed in MySQL

### Mark Completed in MySQL

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Fingerprint for Idempotency · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per lead (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Summarize Run Stats · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (MySQL)

### Append Audit Row (MySQL)

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Outlook · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Send Summary to Outlook

Sends an email via Microsoft Outlook — recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#sales`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Lead Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Nurture subscribers with MySQL and Outlook hands-free

**Pattern:** Drip Sequence · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Nurture subscribers with MySQL and Outlook hands-free. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual capture forms is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Weekly Schedule → Read Pending Queue (Google Sheets) → Stamp & Shape Lead → Payload Valid? → Work in Batches → Aggregate Batch Results → Mark Completed in MySQL → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to drip sequence without manual effort
- Anyone automating capture forms in sales
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; MySQL connection; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The batch size and the Wait between iterations exist specifically to respect the downstream API's rate limit — processing everything as fast as possible just gets the workflow throttled or banned. Marking items completed only after a batch succeeds leaves a clean resume point if the run dies mid-way, instead of an ambiguous half-processed state.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Lower the batch size and raise the wait — measure the throughput/reliability tradeoff directly.
2. Simulate a failed item mid-batch and see exactly what 'Mark Completed' captures.
3. Point the queue read at a different store platform.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your capture forms process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
